---
title: "Research locations"
---

::: {style="text-align: justify;"}
This map shows the locations of our projects, along with brief descriptions of the main research topics and study species. Zoom in on the region of interest and click on the pins to explore individual projects. Additional information is available through the Projects' page and related Publications.
:::

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(leaflet)
library(knitr) # <-- Added this to access the image conversion tool

study_sites <- read.csv("study_species.csv", stringsAsFactors=TRUE) 
study_sites <- na.omit(study_sites)

# 1. Create a color palette function mapped to your specific categories
project_pal <- colorFactor(
  palette = c("darkgreen", "yellow", "orange"),
  domain = c("Completed", "Field completed, ongoing publications", "Ongoing")
)

# 2. NEW STEP: Convert the local image paths to embedded Base64 data
study_sites$img_data <- sapply(as.character(study_sites$image_url), function(path) {
  if (file.exists(path)) {
    return(knitr::image_uri(path))
  } else {
    return("") # Return empty string if the file isn't found
  }
})

# UPDATED STEP: Build popup HTML with responsive image styling
study_sites$popup <- paste0(
  "<b>", study_sites$species, "</b><br>",
  study_sites$description, "<br>",
  ifelse(
    study_sites$img_data == "", 
    "", 
    # Use max-width so it shrinks if needed, and height:auto to keep proportions
    paste0("<img src='", study_sites$img_data, 
           "' style='width: 100%; max-width: 150px; height: auto; display: block; margin-top: 5px; border-radius: 8px;'>")
  )
)

# 4. Create the leaflet map
m <- leaflet(study_sites) %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>%
  
  # Add color-coded pins
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~long,
    lat = ~lat,
    color = ~project_pal(project_type), # Applies the palette directly
    radius = 4,
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    popup = ~popup
  ) %>%
  
  # Add Legend for project types
  addLegend(
    "bottomright",
    pal = project_pal,
    values = ~project_type,
    title = "Project Types",
    opacity = 1
  )

# 5. Fit map bounds to show all study sites globally
m %>% fitBounds(
  lng1 = min(study_sites$long) - 10,
  lat1 = min(study_sites$lat) - 10,
  lng2 = max(study_sites$long) + 10,
  lat2 = max(study_sites$lat) + 10
)

```
